sections.title: Fats

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: can help meals feel more complete and usable. fats sometimes improve the whole plate, not just their own stats. helps meals actually work. a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one. efficient energy source. that can be a strength in the right context.

sections.subtitleText: the upsides first: can help meals feel more complete and usable. fats sometimes improve the whole plate, not just their own stats. helps meals actually work. a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one. efficient energy source. that can be a strength in the right context.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: not a complete food on its own. even the better fats are still just part of a meal. calorie density is brutally high. tiny serving errors matter here. easy to overpour without noticing. practical portion creep is real.

closing.summary: Peanut Oil does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
